characters.name: B.A. Brown
characters.description: Brown's evil twin of sorts. Brown uses camera tricks to appear as himself on one side of the screen and as his "brother" on the other. B.A. is often used in a manner similar to Goofus and Gallant to compare and contrast Brown's and B.A's cooking techniques and their results. In "American Pickle," for example, B.A. compares Brown's sweet pickled fruit to a recipe for extremely hot "Firecracker" carrots. As shown in the episode "Sub Standards," he is Brown's identical twin in the Good Eats universe and can imitate Brown perfectly when he wants to. He is frequently depicted as being a criminal, the exact opposite of Alton.

characters.name: Chuck
characters.description: Chuck is Brown's "Butcher Neighbor." He has appeared in several episodes, such as "Bean Stalker", "Squid Pro Quo", and "A Chuck For Chuck". Chuck also appears in the episode "Romancing the Bird: A Good Eats Thanksgiving," where he drives a "Turkey Truck" and explains the difference between fresh, frozen, and refrigerated turkeys. In "Chops Ahoy," Chuck abducted Brown's charcoal grill 'Fireball' in order to convince Brown to buy a new propane grill.

characters.name: Colonel Bob Boatwright
characters.description: A white-clad Southern gentleman based on Southern Gentlemen of yore, with a distinct resemblance to Colonel Sanders, Colonel Boatwright demonstrates traditional Southern recipes such as upside-down cake, ambrosia, mint julep, catfish, and bourbon pecan pie. While the character is not directly based on the famous KFC Proprietor, Alton Brown himself also holds the honorary title of Kentucky Colonel. According to the Catfish episode, the Colonel claims to be Alton's uncle by way of mentioning "That no-good nephew of mine".

characters.name: Cousin Ray
characters.description: Brown's cousin, who seems to be a bit of a redneck. Ray also appears to be either a con man or simply uneducated when it comes to food. In "Crustacean Nation," Ray claims that the shrimp he is selling are turning pink because they are happy to see Brown when actually, as Brown explains, they are cooking in their shells due to the heat.

characters.name: Elton Brown
characters.description: Marsha's son, hence Brown's nephew. Elton is often featured as Brown's assistant, learning the ways of cooking in the process. Brown treats Elton almost like his own son, despite his and Marsha's constant bickering. Herina, who looks strikingly similar to Brown, also plays a young Brown when necessary, as in the introduction to the roulade-centered episode "Fit to be Tied," and also appeared in the 1996 movie Nightjohn as the character Homer Waller.

characters.name: Farmer McGregor
characters.description: An elderly man who has a farm nearAlton Brown's home, he prides himself on his home-grown produce and enters them regularly in county fair food contests. Brown used to pilfer from McGregor's prizedtomatopatch, but soon stopped when McGregor realized the tomato dishes Brown gave him were made with his own tomatoes. McGregor also has the skills to produce larger-than-average produce, making apotatothat weighed 29 lb (13 kg), although it lost in the "Big Food" contest to Brown's modestolive. McGregor also appears in theeggplantepisode and brings Brown too many eggplants to use, often using wheelbarrows and cardboard boxes as delivery methods. The character is named after the same character in thePeter Rabbitseries of books byBeatrix Potter. Farmer McGregor is portrayed by Bill Greeley.

characters.name: Female Caesar Salad Date
characters.description: TheFemale Caesar Salad Dateis on a date with theMale Caesar Salad Date. She drinks white wine. TheMaitre D'educates them about the origin of caesar salad. He makes the salad tableside, while asking them about their lives. Stephanie Boyd Salad Daze

characters.name: Frances Andersen
characters.description: Frances (a parody of Annie Wilkes from Misery) is Brown's self-proclaimed biggest fan. She has a collection of many of the show's props that she purchased from the internet. Brown first encountered Frances in "This Spud's For You Too" after his truck broke down, and she held Brown hostage in "Ill Gotten Grains" after he lost his memory in a fender-bender.

characters.name: French Oil Vendor
characters.description: TheFrench Oil Vendorrides in a carriage with theFrench Vinegar Maker, bringing a salad to Louis XIV. He thinks vinegar is stinky. They hit a bump and the oil and the vinegar splash into each other. They are mad at each other at first, but then like the taste. Alton Brown Salad Daze

characters.name: French Vinegar Maker
characters.description: TheFrench Vinegar Makerrides in a carriage with theFrench Oil Vendor, bringing a salad to Louis XIV. He thinks oil is greasy. They hit a bump and the oil and the vinegar splash into each other. They are mad at each other at first, but then like the taste. Salad Daze Steve Rooney

characters.name: Husband
characters.description: TheHusbandnotices the Wife can't decide a chicken is done, so they ask an 8 ball together, which says "Ask again later". Patrick Cusick A Bird In The Pan

characters.name: Itchy and Twitchy
characters.description: Itchy and Twitchyappear in several episodes and are Alton Brown's attornies, often appearing when he makes a disclaimer. They never speak, instead they hand Brown sheets of paper. The very first appearance of Itchy and Twitchy was in Season 09, Episode 17, "Tender is the loin II," where they appear as "The Good Eats Legal Team."

characters.name: Koko Karl
characters.description: Karl is the symbol of conglomerate chocolate manufacturers like Hershey. He is a boisterous, pushy, smooth-talking businessman. In the episode "Art of Darkness II: Cocoa", he is seen peddling his products to people on the streets and does his best to force them down people's throats. After encountering Karl, Brown becomes fed up with Karl's inferior products and sets out to produce his own homemade versions. Partway through the episode, an article says that he was tarred and feathered by an angry mob. At the end of the episode, another article says Karl went bankrupt and was indicted for selling inferior chocolate.

characters.name: Lactose Man
characters.description: While appearing to be a superhero, he is in fact a nemesis to Brown, who is lactose intolerant. Lactose Man usually appears in dairy product-based episodes. A variation of the Lactose Man character,Lever Man(albeit in the same costume) appeared in the episode "Shell Game", which is dedicated to oysters. Brown may or may not know the true identity of Lactose Man, as he identified Lever Man as being his apprentice, Paul. The Lever Man costume was revamped for use as Lactose Man. In real life, Brown is not lactose intolerant, which he revealed in "Breakfast Eats II".

characters.name: Maitre D'
characters.description: TheMaitre D'educates two people on a date about the origin of caesar salad. He makes the salad tableside, while asking them about their lives. Alton Brown Salad Daze

characters.name: Male Caesar Salad Date
characters.description: TheMale Caesar Salad Dateis on a date with theFemale Caesar Salad Date. The Maitre D' educates them about the origin of caesar salad. He makes the salad tableside, while asking them about their lives. He thinks the salad includes anchovys, but is corrected. John Crow Salad Daze

characters.name: Marsha Brown-Brady
characters.description: Brown's older sister. Marsha is a constant source of annoyance to Brown (and, as revealed in some episodes, to members of the Good Eats production staff also). She often tricks or cajoles Brown into cooking for her, which usually initiates the topic of that episode. For instance, in "Circle of Life," Marsha manages to talk Brown into making dozens of doughnuts for her Bunny Scout troop's bake sale, and then tricks him into buying them all back. She has been divorced more than once, as Brown referred to her ink not having dried on her LAST divorce "yet" in "Behind the Bird."

characters.name: Mime
characters.description: He juggles potatoes at one time. John Gregario This Spud's for You

characters.name: Paul
characters.description: Paul is Brown's apprentice and intern. Paul is generally incompetent and quite often seems to stress out regarding food-related issues. Brown often uses Paul as a human guinea pig for demonstrations.

characters.name: Santa Claus
characters.description: Santa as he appears in "The Cookie Clause". Santa Clausis a popular figure in pop culture, known as an individual who travels around the world on Christmas Eve and delivers presents to children. In the world of Good Eats, he first appears in the episode "The Cookie Clause", in which he blackmails Alton Brown into creating cookies for him, threatening to put him on his "Naughty List" shall he refuse. He later appears in "Twas the Night before Good Eats", this time as "Sinta Claus", where he convinces Alton to create "Wassle" for the carolers outside. In the show, he is also shown to have the ability to manipulate time.

characters.name: Santa Claus
characters.description: Santa as he appears in "The Cookie Clause". Santa Clausis a popular figure in pop culture, known as an individual who travels around the world on Christmas Eve and delivers presents to children. In the world of Good Eats, he first appears in the episode "The Cookie Clause", in which he blackmails Alton Brown into creating cookies for him, threatening to put him on his "Naughty List" shall he refuse. He later appears in "Twas the Night before Good Eats", this time as "Sinta Claus", where he convinces Alton to create "Wassle" for the carolers outside. In the show, he is also shown to have the ability to manipulate time.

characters.name: Scrap Iron Chef
characters.description: AB’s the challenger ... and bacon’s the name of the game. Learn how you can—and why you should—make your own bacon. And how to cook it right too. Get the skinny on pork bellies and learn how to make a smoking device with junk from your own backyard. Pepper(Black pepper)  Chef Bayou- This is a Scrap Iron Chef. Chef Bayou has had 57 bouts and 57 victories. His secret ingredient is White Wine. Chef Southwest- This is a Scrap Iron Chef. Chef Southwest has had 21 bouts and 21 victories. His secret ingredient is chiles ... lots of chilies. Chef Prairie- This is a Scrap Iron Chef. Chef Prairie has had 172 bouts and 172 victories. Her her secret ingredient is lots of love. Scrap Iron Chairman
characters.personality_section: Chef Bayou- This is a Scrap Iron Chef. Chef Bayou has had 57 bouts and 57 victories. His secret ingredient is White Wine. Chef Southwest- This is a Scrap Iron Chef. Chef Southwest has had 21 bouts and 21 victories. His secret ingredient is chiles ... lots of chilies. Chef Prairie- This is a Scrap Iron Chef. Chef Prairie has had 172 bouts and 172 victories. Her her secret ingredient is lots of love. Scrap Iron Chairman

characters.name: Short Order Cook
characters.description: TheShort Order Cookis very angry. He rolls up his sleeves and has a pencil on his ear. Alton shows him how to detect a raw egg via spinning. The cook smashes the raw one. Steve Rooney The Egg-Files

characters.name: Sid Maxburg
characters.description: Sid is a bombastic entertainment agent who is usually seen trying to revamp allegedly forgotten foods like vanilla ("My Pod"), sweet potatoes ("Potato, My Sweet"), and okra ("Okraphobia").

characters.name: Stranger with Chicken
characters.description: John Crow A Bird In The Pan

characters.name: The Dungeon Master
characters.description: Brown's personal dungeon master (a parody of Igor and Sméagol), who appears in 'Cubing A Round', 'Tort(illa) Reform' and 'There Will Be Oil'. Brown usually disapproves of his loose grip on reality; for example, in 'Cubing A Round' he spends $1,500 on a Swiss mechanical steak cuber using "the little plastic thing with the numbers on it" (a credit card). “Seedsies.” (Arils) ‘Fruit 10 From Outer Space’.

characters.name: The Lady of the Refrigerator
characters.description: The Lady of the Refrigerator(a parody of The Lady of the Lake), is a character occasionally appears inAlton Brown's refrigerator to impart information about the food or cooking technique covered in the episode, and to tease and needle him. The Lady of the Refrigerator is portrayed by dieticianCarolyn O'Neil.

characters.name: The Lady of the Refrigerator
characters.description: The Lady of the Refrigerator(a parody of The Lady of the Lake), is a character occasionally appears inAlton Brown's refrigerator to impart information about the food or cooking technique covered in the episode, and to tease and needle him. The Lady of the Refrigerator is portrayed by dieticianCarolyn O'Neil.

characters.name: The Mad French Chef
characters.description: Brown's arch-nemesis, who is stereotypically snobbish toward all non-French forms of cooking, and berates Brown for not using French techniques. He says you should tear greens instead of cutting them, to prevent brown edges. Over the course of the series, the Chef seems to become more angry, as his title in different episodes changes to "Really Mad French Chef" and beyond. The Chef is currently voiced by Brown, as he is no longer seen on-screen except for one "appearance" as an oven mitt/puppet in the episode "Crepe Expectations". Steve Rooney

characters.name: The Mad French Chef
characters.description: Brown's arch-nemesis, who is stereotypically snobbish toward all non-French forms of cooking, and berates Brown for not using French techniques. He says you should tear greens instead of cutting them, to prevent brown edges. Over the course of the series, the Chef seems to become more angry, as his title in different episodes changes to "Really Mad French Chef" and beyond. The Chef is currently voiced by Brown, as he is no longer seen on-screen except for one "appearance" as an oven mitt/puppet in the episode "Crepe Expectations". Steve Rooney

characters.name: Thing
characters.description: Named after Thing from The Addams Family, Thing is a hand that appears in random locations to hand Brown ingredients and tools, to which Brown replies, "Thank you, Thing." In "Behind the Eats," it was "revealed" that Brown's Thing is the son of the Addams Family Thing.

characters.name: USDA Agents
characters.description: USDA agents are characters that randomly appear, usually when Brown is talking about internal temperatures of meat that disagree with the USDA, or the requirements for a food item to be labeled a certain way. Brown actually plays the one in the center, using camera tricks, and cast members play the other two agents that often accompany him. They are sometimes refered to as "feds".

characters.name: USDA Inspector
characters.description: AUSDA Inspectorappears whenever Alton is about to violate one of the USDA's codes. Their appearence is similar to FBI agents, wearing black suits, sunglasses, and ear monitors. The speak in technical jargon. Steak Your Claim

characters.name: W
characters.description: A parody of James Bond's "Q",Wis a manager at stores where Brown goes to find kitchen gear. W is antisocial, sardonic, and finds Brown very annoying. Brown realizes this, and intentionally fools with gadgets and acts silly to get a rise out of her. He also likes to sabotage her attempts to make sales. Even so, she is the most reliable source of equipment for Brown, as she knows everything about kitchen tools and appliances. She informs Alton that a spinner is the best way to dry greens fast. Different makes and models have different drive mechanisms. She states that the best use a spring loaded Archimedes screw with a push button and a brake. She tells Alton a chicken in a baking pan would be better off roasting in a hub cap off his father's Oldsmobile. The right roasting pan evenly distributes, absorbs and reflects heat and can be placed over two burners for fast browning and sauce reduction. It should be heavy, have upright sturdy handles to hold with mits, and have a polished surface. This reflects heat and holds on to pan drippings, not possible on a non-stick surface. She hands Alton a pan large enouth for a rib roast but not too big for lasagna. Alton almost takes her mitts.

characters.name: Wife
characters.description: TheWifecan't decide if her chicken is so she asks an 8 ball with the Husband, which says to "Ask again later". Tara Burtchaell A Bird In The Pan

characters.name: Yumion
characters.description: Yumion is the real life mascot for Vidalia, Georgia. He likes to prance through onion fields with Alton.
